178.reason: [After handling a single '/', pos is decremented, but will be incremented again at the end of the loop, causing correct advancement. However, the real issue is that the second 'else if' for closing '*/' only triggers when inside a comment AND curChar is '*', but if a line ends with '*' and next line starts with '/', the multi-line comment closure may be missed because line-by-line processing breaks context. But more critically, the main error relevant to the test case is not here.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 1.0 , reason:  [After handling a single '/', pos is decremented, but will be incremented again at the end of the loop, causing correct advancement. However, the real issue is that the second 'else if' for closing '*/' only triggers when inside a comment AND curChar is '*', but if a line ends with '*' and next line starts with '/', the multi-line comment closure may be missed because line-by-line processing breaks context. But more critically, the main error relevant to the test case is not here.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 1.0 , reason:  [After incrementing pos at the start of handling '/', if the next character is not '/' or '*', the code decrements pos to reprocess the current '/' as a DIV token. However, the outer loop will then increment pos again, causing the '/' to be skipped entirely when it should be consumed as a token. This leads to incorrect parsing or infinite loops in some cases.]
